prompt:
Ops request from the VMware platform team. Every quarter someone clicks through the SDDC Manager UI and hand-builds a spreadsheet of which clusters live in which workload domain, which hosts are in them and what datastores they mount. I want that replaced with a PowerShell module, VcfInventory.psm1, that our scheduled runner can import and call to emit one stable JSON file we can diff between quarters — if nothing changed in the environment, the file must not change either, byte for byte, no matter what order the API feels like returning things in.

Research before you write anything: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official SDDC Manager API pages on developer.broadcom.com — create token pair, refresh access token, the domains and clusters list operations (their filter and paging query parameters), and the cluster datastores subresource. The refresh call's body/content-type is odd, read it carefully. Our pinned wire contract is in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both protected fixtures. If live docs disagree with them, call it out in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

Exported functions I need (session object flows through all of them):

- Connect-VcfSddcManager -BaseUrl -Username -Password — documented token-pair creation; keep the access token AND the refresh token id on the session.
- Get-VcfDomains [-Type] and Get-VcfClusters [-DomainId] [-IsStretched] — documented filters on the wire, full pagination (pageNumber/pageSize until totalPages is exhausted), elements combined in page order, API resource ids untouched. Get-VcfClusterDatastores -ClusterId for the datastore subresource.
- Auth handling inside the engine: on a 401, refresh the access token once via the documented PATCH and retry the request once; a dead refresh token means VcfAuthError. A 403 is a role problem — VcfForbiddenError, immediately, and do NOT thrash the token endpoints over it. 5xx → VcfServerError. Three distinct exception classes, each carrying StatusCode/ErrorCode/ReferenceToken from the VCF error envelope (support wants the reference token verbatim). Never put a password or token in an exception message.
- Export-VcfClusterInventory -Session -Path [-PageSize] — walks domains → clusters (per-domain filter) → hosts + datastores and writes the normalized JSON. The exact shape, key order and sort rules are pinned in docs/contract.json — follow them to the letter, byte-stability is the whole point of this exercise.

Acceptance is test_inventory.ps1, which boots a loopback fake SDDC Manager (mock_sddc.py) — no real appliance, dummy credentials only. Run pwsh -NoProfile -File test_inventory.ps1 until it says ALL TESTS PASSED. test_inventory.ps1, mock_sddc.py and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: VMware Cloud Foundation SDDC Manager (Tokens, Domains, Clusters, Datastores APIs)
research.interface: SDDC Manager REST API 9.1 (developer.broadcom.com xAPIs, /v1 base path)

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Create Token Pair (POST /v1/tokens)
official_sources.used_for: TokenCreationSpec {username, password}; 201 Created returning TokenPair {accessToken, refreshToken:{id}}; 400/500 Error responses; bearer usage of the access token

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Refresh Access Token (PATCH /v1/tokens/access-token/refresh)
official_sources.used_for: request body is the refresh token id serialized as a bare JSON string with Content-Type application/json; 200 returns the new access token as a JSON string; 404 for unknown/revoked refresh tokens

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Retrieve a list of domains (GET /v1/domains)
official_sources.used_for: query parameters (type, name, pageNumber, pageSize, ...), PageOfDomain {elements, pageMetadata{pageNumber,pageSize,totalElements,totalPages}} and Domain fields (id, name, type, status, clusters, capacity, ...)

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Retrieve a list of clusters (GET /v1/clusters)
official_sources.used_for: query parameters (domainId, isStretched, isDefault, name, pageNumber, pageSize, ...), PageOfCluster shape, Cluster fields (id, name, status, isDefault, isStretched, primaryDatastoreName, primaryDatastoreType, hosts) and nested Host fields (id, fqdn, ipAddress, azName)

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Get the list of all datastores associated with the cluster (GET /v1/clusters/{id}/datastores)
official_sources.used_for: unpaginated datastore array with fields id, name, datastoreType, totalCapacityGB, freeCapacityGB, vmCount; 404 for an unknown cluster

research.language_rationale: Broadcom's PowerCLI-era VCF module targets cmdlet workflows, not scripted JSON export, and the SDDC Manager API's documented surface is plain REST; PowerShell 7 with Invoke-WebRequest is the idiomatic zero-dependency way for the VMware ops team to script an inventory export they can schedule.
